title: Cosmologist Genres
alt: Inflationary cosmologists call all music from after the first 10^-30 seconds "post-"
transcript:
Music genres according to...

...Non-cosmologists	...Cosmologists
Pop                	Lite
Rock               	Metal
Hip hop            	Metal
Metal              	Metal
Country            	Metal
Dance/Electronic   	Metal
Latin              	Metal
Punk               	Metal
Classical          	Metal
Jazz               	Metal
Folk               	Metal
explanation:
A cosmologist is someone who studies the universe; cosmology is a branch of astronomy. When describing the composition of stars in astronomical terms,  are all the elements heavier than . This definition of metal is significantly broader than . Likewise, this chart of musical genres describes everything "heavier" than  as metal. The standard conception of elemental metals is a subset of the astronomical conception of metals; likewise, here the musical genre  is presented as a subset of the genres cosmologists consider metal.

Randall may have decided to portray pop music in a similar way to the elements helium and hydrogen as a reference to the "", the test for hydrogen as a product of a chemical reaction. Possibly simultaneously, he may be thinking of how one might "pop" a helium balloon, thus the "pop" analogy pulls double duty as a reference for both elements. 

Cosmologists also study the history and future of the universe, and the title text refers to the . At roughly 10-32 seconds after the Big Bang, the  ended, causing a large number of , and  to come into existence. In , this point is considered to be the end of the Big Bang. Randall jokingly refers to it as "post-" because nearly the entire history of the universe is after this instant. This is a reference to types of music with "post-" in their names, e.g. , , .
